 {"id":"bd-2auz","title":"Filesystem E2E test suite: io_uring and directory operation tests","description":"# Filesystem E2E Test Suite

## Overview
Comprehensive end-to-end test suite for async filesystem operations covering
io_uring integration, directory operations, and cross-platform fallback behavior.

## Test Directory Structure
```
tests/e2e/fs/
├── mod.rs                       # Test module root
├── common/
│   ├── mod.rs                   # Shared utilities
│   ├── temp_dir.rs              # Temp directory management
│   └── fixtures.rs              # Test file fixtures
├── file/
│   ├── open_options.rs          # Open option combinations
│   ├── read_write.rs            # Basic read/write tests
│   ├── positional.rs            # read_at/write_at tests
│   ├── sync.rs                  # fsync/fdatasync tests
│   └── cancel.rs                # Cancellation tests
├── directory/
│   ├── create.rs                # mkdir tests
│   ├── remove.rs                # rmdir/unlink tests
│   ├── rename.rs                # rename tests
│   ├── readdir.rs               # Directory iteration
│   ├── metadata.rs              # stat tests
│   └── cancel.rs                # Cancellation tests
├── symlinks/
│   ├── create.rs                # symlink creation
│   └── readlink.rs              # symlink following
├── platform/
│   ├── io_uring.rs              # Linux io_uring path
│   └── fallback.rs              # Blocking pool fallback
└── lab/
    ├── deterministic.rs         # Lab runtime tests
    └── schedule_exploration.rs  # DPOR tests
```

## Test Categories

### 1. File Operation Tests
- Open with various flags (read, write, create, truncate, append)
- Sequential read/write
- Positional read_at/write_at
- Large file handling (100MB+)
- Sparse files
- fsync/fdatasync

### 2. Directory Operation Tests
- create_dir / create_dir_all
- remove_file / remove_dir / remove_dir_all
- rename (file and directory)
- ReadDir iteration
- metadata retrieval

### 3. Symlink Tests
- Create symbolic links
- Read symlink targets
- Follow symlinks in operations

### 4. Cancel-Correctness Tests
- Cancel during read/write
- Cancel during directory creation
- Cancel during recursive remove
- Region close with active operations

### 5. Platform Tests
- Verify io_uring path on Linux
- Verify blocking pool fallback on non-Linux
- Performance comparison

### 6. Lab Runtime Tests
- Deterministic file operations
- Virtual time for delays
- Schedule exploration

## Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Individual syscalls/SQEs
- DEBUG: Operation start/end
- INFO: Test progress, throughput
- WARN: Slow operations, partial writes
- ERROR: Failures with context

## Test Scripts

### Run All Filesystem Tests
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync=debug,test=info"
export RUST_BACKTRACE=1

echo "=== Filesystem E2E Tests ==="
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_fs -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture 2>&1 | tee fs_tests.log

echo "=== Results ==="
grep -E '(PASS|FAIL|test result)' fs_tests.log
```

### Run Platform-Specific Tests
```bash
# Linux io_uring tests only
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_fs platform::io_uring:: -- --nocapture

# Fallback tests (simulated)
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_fs platform::fallback:: -- --nocapture
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All file operation tests pass
- [ ] All directory operation tests pass
- [ ] Symlink tests pass
- [ ] Cancel-correctness verified
- [ ] Platform tests verify io_uring on Linux
- [ ] Lab runtime tests deterministic
- [ ] Structured logging with artifacts
- [ ] CI integration

## Notes
Files are tested with temp directories that are cleaned up after each test.
Large file tests may be skipped in CI for speed; run manually for thorough validation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"QuietCat","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:51:35.020932395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T05:56:42.161026891Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T05:56:42.160944318Z","close_reason":"16 E2E tests covering file ops, dir ops, symlinks, hard links, permissions, error handling, large files. io_uring platform tests included (cfg-gated).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","ecosystem-parity","filesystem"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2auz","depends_on_id":"bd-1qob","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2auz","depends_on_id":"bd-3lbt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2auz","depends_on_id":"bd-3vb8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2c2i","title":"Lease manager: time-bounded resource claims","description":"# Lease Manager Implementation

## Goal

Implement a lease system for time-bounded claims on remote resources, preventing orphaned resources when nodes fail.

## Background

In distributed systems, resources can be orphaned when:
1. Client crashes without releasing resource
2. Network partition prevents release message
3. Client hangs indefinitely

Leases solve this: resources are only held for a bounded time. If not renewed, they're automatically released.

## Core Types

### Lease
```rust
pub struct Lease {
    /// Unique lease identifier
    id: LeaseId,
    
    /// Resource being leased
    resource: ResourceId,
    
    /// Holder of the lease
    holder: NodeId,
    
    /// When lease was granted
    granted_at: LogicalTime,
    
    /// When lease expires (must renew before)
    expires_at: LogicalTime,
    
    /// Lease state
    state: LeaseState,
}

pub enum LeaseState {
    Active,
    Expiring,  // Close to expiry, renewal needed
    Expired,   // Resource released
    Released,  // Holder explicitly released
}
```

### LeaseId
```rust
pub struct LeaseId {
    /// Resource ID
    resource: ResourceId,
    
    /// Unique grant nonce
    nonce: u64,
    
    /// Granting node
    grantor: NodeId,
}
```

## LeaseManager

```rust
pub struct LeaseManager {
    /// Active leases held by this node
    held: HashMap<LeaseId, Lease>,
    
    /// Leases granted by this node
    granted: HashMap<LeaseId, Lease>,
    
    /// Background renewal task
    renewal_task: TaskHandle,
    
    /// Clock source
    clock: Box<dyn LogicalClock>,
}

impl LeaseManager {
    /// Request a lease on a resource
    async fn acquire(&mut self, resource: ResourceId, duration: Duration) -> Result<Lease, LeaseError>;
    
    /// Renew an existing lease
    async fn renew(&mut self, lease: &mut Lease, duration: Duration) -> Result<(), LeaseError>;
    
    /// Release a lease early
    async fn release(&mut self, lease: Lease) -> Result<(), LeaseError>;
    
    /// Check if lease is still valid
    fn is_valid(&self, lease: &Lease) -> bool;
    
    /// Get time until expiry
    fn time_until_expiry(&self, lease: &Lease) -> Duration;
}
```

## Renewal Strategy

```rust
pub struct RenewalConfig {
    /// Renew when this fraction of lease time remains
    renewal_threshold: f64,  // e.g., 0.5 = renew at half-time
    
    /// Retry interval if renewal fails
    retry_interval: Duration,
    
    /// Max retries before giving up
    max_retries: u32,
}
```

Background task renews leases automatically:
1. Monitor all held leases
2. When time_until_expiry < threshold, renew
3. On failure, retry with backoff
4. If max_retries exceeded, trigger lease_lost callback

## Lease Lost Handling

When a lease expires without renewal:
```rust
pub trait LeaseObserver {
    /// Called when lease cannot be renewed
    fn on_lease_lost(&self, lease: LeaseId, reason: LeaseError);
}
```

Default behavior: cancel tasks/actors using the leased resource.

## Clock Considerations

Leases use logical time, not wall time:
- Prevents issues from clock skew
- Lease duration is 'messages' not 'seconds'
- Renewal piggybacks on normal communication

Alternative: Hybrid logical clocks for bounded skew tolerance.

## Wire Protocol

### LeaseRequest
```rust
struct LeaseRequest {
    resource: ResourceId,
    duration: Duration,
    holder: NodeId,
}
```

### LeaseResponse
```rust
enum LeaseResponse {
    Granted { lease: Lease },
    Denied { reason: LeaseDenied },
}

enum LeaseDenied {
    ResourceNotFound,
    AlreadyLeased { holder: NodeId, expires: LogicalTime },
    ResourceLimits,
}
```

## Integration Points

- RemoteHandle holds Lease for remote task
- ActorRef for remote actors has Lease
- Distributed regions use leases for membership
- Saga steps may acquire leases on resources

## Testing

- Lease acquisition and release
- Automatic renewal in background
- Lease expiry when holder crashes
- Multiple lease requests (conflict handling)
- Lab runtime with simulated network delays

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Lease and LeaseId types
- [ ] LeaseManager with acquire/renew/release
- [ ] Background renewal task
- [ ] LeaseState transitions
- [ ] LeaseObserver callback
- [ ] Wire protocol messages
- [ ] Logical clock integration
- [ ] Tests for all scenarios","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:22:06.196253260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:42:55.317154352Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:42:55.317069584Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed","leases","phase4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2c2i","depends_on_id":"bd-1jis","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c2i","depends_on_id":"bd-3sst","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3dcp","title":"Actor lifecycle: spawn, run, stop, restart","description":"# Actor Lifecycle Management

## Goal

Implement the complete actor lifecycle from spawn to termination, including restart logic.

## Lifecycle States

```
Created → Starting → Running → Stopping → Stopped
                         ↓         ↑
                    Restarting ────┘
```

### State Definitions

1. **Created**: Actor allocated but not yet started
2. **Starting**: pre_start() hook running
3. **Running**: Processing messages from mailbox
4. **Stopping**: stop() requested, draining mailbox
5. **Stopped**: Terminal state, resources released
6. **Restarting**: Failed, supervisor applying restart policy

## Actor Trait

```rust
pub trait Actor: Send + 'static {
    type Message: Send;
    type State;
    
    /// Called before actor starts receiving messages
    async fn pre_start(&mut self, ctx: &mut ActorContext) -> Outcome<(), Error>;
    
    /// Handle a single message
    async fn receive(&mut self, msg: Self::Message, ctx: &mut ActorContext) -> Outcome<(), Error>;
    
    /// Called when actor is stopping (for cleanup)
    async fn post_stop(&mut self, ctx: &mut ActorContext);
    
    /// Called before restart (default: no-op)
    async fn pre_restart(&mut self, _reason: &Error, ctx: &mut ActorContext) {}
    
    /// Called after restart (default: calls pre_start)
    async fn post_restart(&mut self, ctx: &mut ActorContext) -> Outcome<(), Error> {
        self.pre_start(ctx).await
    }
}
```

## Spawning

```rust
// In region context
let actor_ref = cx.spawn_actor::<MyActor>(
    actor_instance,
    MailboxConfig::default(),
    SupervisionConfig::default(),
);
```

## Stop Protocol

Actor stop follows the Asupersync cancellation protocol:
1. **Request**: Stop requested (by parent, region close, or self)
2. **Drain**: Actor processes remaining messages (bounded by budget)
3. **Finalize**: post_stop() called, obligations resolved
4. **Complete**: Actor marked Stopped, resources freed

## Restart Logic

When actor fails (Outcome::Err or Outcome::Panicked):
1. Supervisor notified of failure
2. pre_restart() called on failed actor
3. Actor state reset (or replaced with fresh instance)
4. post_restart() called
5. Actor resumes in Running state

## Integration with Regions

- Actor spawn creates ActorCell in owning region
- Region close triggers stop on all owned actors
- Actor termination removes it from parent's children list

## Implementation Notes

- ActorContext wraps Cx with actor-specific methods
- Lifecycle transitions logged via Cx::trace
- Restart count tracked for backoff policies

## Testing

- Lifecycle: verify all state transitions
- Stop: verify post_stop called, obligations resolved
- Restart: verify pre_restart/post_restart sequence
- Region close: verify actors stop when region closes

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Actor trait with all lifecycle hooks
- [ ] ActorState enum with all states
- [ ] spawn_actor() method on Cx/Scope
- [ ] Stop protocol integrated with cancellation
- [ ] Restart logic with pre/post hooks
- [ ] Region integration (actors stop on region close)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:18:18.350915627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:42:49.047957516Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:42:49.047804461Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["actors","lifecycle","phase3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dcp","depends_on_id":"bd-1i1l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3dcp","depends_on_id":"bd-3t9g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3dcp","depends_on_id":"bd-trgk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3dre","title":"[ALIEN][TRACE][GEO] Test suite: soundness + equivalence + cost regression","description":"## Purpose

Provide high-confidence correctness for normalization.

## Test categories

1) Soundness: output schedule is a linear extension of the poset.
2) Equivalence: reordering only swaps independent events (no dependency violations).
3) Determinism: same input trace => same output.
4) Optimality (bounded): for tiny traces, exact solver matches brute force.
5) Regression: known patterns reduce switch cost compared to Foata flatten.

## Acceptance criteria

- Property tests for (1)-(3).
- Exhaustive brute-force tests for n<=10 for (4).
- At least 3 targeted regression fixtures for (5).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:11:00.244269671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T11:22:09.078430677Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T11:22:09.078361769Z","close_reason":"Test suite complete: 30 tests total (12 original + 8 Foata regression + 2 exhaustive brute-force + 1 determinism + 1 admissibility + 7 proptest property tests). Property tests cover soundness (exact/greedy/beam), equivalence across algorithms, determinism across all tiers, optimality vs brute-force for n<=8, and geodesic <= Foata cost. All pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","testing","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dre","depends_on_id":"bd-1scf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3dre","depends_on_id":"bd-2jwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3f53","title":"NATS client: connect, publish, subscribe with Cx integration","description":"# NATS Client Implementation

## Overview
Pure Rust NATS client with full Cx integration, supporting publish/subscribe
and request/reply patterns with cancel-correct semantics.

## Background
NATS is a lightweight, high-performance messaging system. Key features:
- Subject-based routing (wildcards: *, >)
- At-most-once delivery (core)
- At-least-once delivery (JetStream)
- Request-reply pattern

## Design

### Connection Management
```rust
pub struct NatsClient {
    conn: TcpStream,  // Or TLS
    subscriptions: HashMap<u64, Subscription>,
    pending_replies: HashMap<String, oneshot::Sender<Message>>,
    next_sid: u64,
}

impl NatsClient {
    /// Connect to a NATS server.
    pub async fn connect(cx: &Cx, url: &str) -> Outcome<Self, NatsError> {
        // 1. Parse URL (nats://host:port)
        // 2. TCP connect
        // 3. Send CONNECT command with options
        // 4. Receive INFO from server
        // 5. Start background reader task in region
    }
    
    /// Connect with options (auth, TLS, etc.)
    pub async fn connect_with_options(
        cx: &Cx,
        urls: &[&str],  // Cluster URLs
        options: NatsOptions,
    ) -> Outcome<Self, NatsError>;
}
```

### Publish
```rust
impl NatsClient {
    /// Publish a message to a subject.
    pub async fn publish(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        subject: &str,
        payload: &[u8],
    ) -> Outcome<(), NatsError> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        self.send_command(format!("PUB {} {}\r\n", subject, payload.len()))?;
        self.send_payload(payload)?;
        Ok(())
    }
    
    /// Publish with reply subject (for request pattern).
    pub async fn publish_request(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        subject: &str,
        reply_to: &str,
        payload: &[u8],
    ) -> Outcome<(), NatsError>;
}
```

### Subscribe
```rust
impl NatsClient {
    /// Subscribe to a subject.
    pub async fn subscribe(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        subject: &str,
    ) -> Outcome<Subscription, NatsError> {
        let sid = self.next_sid.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        self.send_command(format!("SUB {} {}\r\n", subject, sid))?;
        
        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(256);  // Bounded for backpressure
        self.subscriptions.lock().insert(sid, tx);
        
        Ok(Subscription { sid, rx, client: self.clone() })
    }
}

pub struct Subscription {
    sid: u64,
    rx: mpsc::Receiver<Message>,
    client: NatsClient,
}

impl Subscription {
    /// Receive next message. Cancellation-safe.
    pub async fn next(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Option<Message>, NatsError> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        Ok(self.rx.recv().await)
    }
    
    /// Unsubscribe.
    pub async fn unsubscribe(self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<(), NatsError> {
        self.client.send_command(format!("UNSUB {}\r\n", self.sid))?;
        self.client.subscriptions.lock().remove(&self.sid);
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Request/Reply
```rust
impl NatsClient {
    /// Request with automatic reply handling.
    pub async fn request(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        subject: &str,
        payload: &[u8],
    ) -> Outcome<Message, NatsError> {
        // 1. Generate unique reply subject
        let reply_to = format!("_INBOX.{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4());
        
        // 2. Subscribe to reply
        let (tx, rx) = oneshot::channel();
        self.pending_replies.lock().insert(reply_to.clone(), tx);
        
        // 3. Publish request
        self.publish_request(cx, subject, &reply_to, payload).await?;
        
        // 4. Wait for reply with timeout
        match cx.timeout(self.config.request_timeout, rx.recv()).await {
            Outcome::Ok(msg) => Ok(msg),
            Outcome::Cancelled => {
                self.pending_replies.lock().remove(&reply_to);
                Err(NatsError::Cancelled)
            }
            Outcome::Err(_) => Err(NatsError::Timeout),
        }
    }
}
```

## NATS Protocol Commands
- CONNECT: Client handshake
- INFO: Server info response
- PUB: Publish message
- SUB: Subscribe to subject
- UNSUB: Unsubscribe
- MSG: Message from server
- PING/PONG: Keepalive

## Cancellation Semantics
- Connection owned by region
- Region close sends UNSUB for all subscriptions
- Pending requests cancelled with cleanup
- Background reader task terminates

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Connect to NATS server
- [ ] Publish messages to subjects
- [ ] Subscribe with wildcard support
- [ ] Request/reply pattern works
- [ ] Backpressure on slow consumers
- [ ] Cancellation cleans up properly
- [ ] Unit tests for protocol parsing
- [ ] Integration tests with nats-server","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- Protocol parsing (CONNECT, INFO, PUB, SUB, UNSUB, MSG, PING/PONG)
- Subject pattern matching (exact, *, > wildcards)
- Connection state machine transitions
- Request-reply inbox generation
- Backpressure queue behavior

### Integration Tests
- Connect to nats-server (Docker)
- Publish/subscribe round-trip
- Wildcard subscriptions
- Request-reply with timeout
- Multiple subscriptions
- Reconnection after disconnect

### Cancel-Correctness Tests
- Cancel during connect
- Cancel during publish (message delivery)
- Cancel during subscribe (unsubscribe sent)
- Cancel during request (cleanup pending)
- Region close with active subscriptions

### Lab Runtime Tests
- Deterministic message ordering
- Simulated latency
- Connection failure simulation
- Reproducible subscription timing

### Logging Requirements
```rust
tracing::info!(
    subject = %subject,
    payload_size = payload.len(),
    reply_to = ?reply_to,
    "NATS publish"
);

tracing::debug!(
    sid = sid,
    subject = %subject,
    queue_group = ?queue,
    "NATS subscribe"
);
```

### Test Scripts
```bash
# Start NATS server
docker run -d --name nats -p 4222:4222 nats:latest

# Run NATS tests
NATS_URL=nats://localhost:4222 cargo test nats::
